verses.1: The plans of the heart belong to man,
verses.2: All the ways of a man are clean in his own eyes,
verses.3: Commit your deeds to the LORD,
verses.4: The LORD has made everything for its own end—
verses.5: Everyone who is proud in heart is an abomination to the LORD;
verses.6: By mercy and truth iniquity is atoned for.
verses.7: When a man’s ways please the LORD,
verses.8: Better is a little with righteousness,
verses.9: A man’s heart plans his course,
verses.10: Inspired judgments are on the lips of the king.
verses.11: Honest balances and scales are the LORD’s;
verses.12: It is an abomination for kings to do wrong,
verses.13: Righteous lips are the delight of kings.
verses.14: The king’s wrath is a messenger of death,
verses.15: In the light of the king’s face is life.
verses.16: How much better it is to get wisdom than gold!
verses.17: The highway of the upright is to depart from evil.
verses.19: It is better to be of a lowly spirit with the poor,
verses.20: He who heeds the Word finds prosperity.
verses.21: The wise in heart shall be called prudent.
verses.22: Understanding is a fountain of life to one who has it,
verses.23: The heart of the wise instructs his mouth,
verses.25: There is a way which seems right to a man,
verses.26: The appetite of the laboring man labors for him,
verses.28: A perverse man stirs up strife.
verses.29: A man of violence entices his neighbor,
verses.30: One who winks his eyes to plot perversities,
verses.31: Gray hair is a crown of glory.
verses.32: One who is slow to anger is better than the mighty;
verses.33: The lot is cast into the lap,
